games.title: Cyberpunk 2077
games.review: An absolutely immersive open world. Night City feels alive and the story kept me hooked till the very end. The Phantom Liberty DLC is a masterpiece.

games.title: Elden Ring
games.review: The most rewarding game I have ever played. Every boss feels like a mountain to climb.

games.title: Hollow Knight: Silksong
games.review: The sequel a decade of players have been waiting for. Hornet takes center stage and I can't wait to dive in.

games.title: The Witcher 3
games.review: A benchmark for open world RPGs. The side quests alone are better than most games' main stories.

games.title: Hollow Knight
games.review: Stunning art direction and brutal but fair combat. The lore is incredibly deep for an indie title.

games.title: Baldur's Gate 3
games.review: Currently in Act 2. This game is redefining what RPGs can be. Every decision feels meaningful.

games.title: Sekiro
games.review: The most satisfying combat system in any game. Learning the rhythm of each boss is pure zen.

games.title: Death Stranding
games.review: A divisive but unforgettable experience. The themes of connection hit differently post-pandemic.

games.title: Control
games.review: Remedy's best work. The brutalist architecture and New Weird storytelling are unlike anything else.

games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
games.review: The game that redefined what open world design could be. Long overdue on my list.

games.title: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
games.review: A direct sequel that reportedly expands on BotW in every direction. The Ultrahand system looks incredible.

games.title: Halo: Combat Evolved
games.review: The one that started it all. A genre-defining classic I've somehow never properly played through.

games.title: Halo 2
games.review: The introduction of the Arbiter and a sharper multiplayer formula. Next on the Master Chief run.

games.title: Halo 3
games.review: The original Chief trilogy's grand finale. I hear the final level still hits.

games.title: Halo 4
games.review: 343's first swing at the series. Curious to see how the Prometheans and the new art direction land.

games.title: Halo 5: Guardians
games.review: The divisive middle entry. Want to play it myself before forming an opinion.

games.title: Halo Infinite
games.review: The return to form. A semi-open Zeta Halo has me very interested.

games.title: Splatoon 2
games.review: Nintendo's take on the shooter genre. The ink-paint mechanic is pure game design.

games.title: Splatoon 3
games.review: The latest iteration with Salmon Run and a refined single-player campaign.

games.title: Outer Wilds
games.review: Every single person I trust with game recommendations says this is a once-in-a-lifetime experience. Going in blind.

games.title: Horizon Zero Dawn
games.review: Post-apocalyptic robot dinosaurs. I don't need much more of a pitch than that.

games.title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
games.review: The one RPG I've somehow never finished. This is the year I finally take the arrow to the knee.

games.title: Portal 2
games.review: Widely regarded as one of the best-written games ever made. Overdue for a full playthrough.

games.title: Terraria
games.review: A deceptively deep 2D sandbox. Friends have told me this game will consume hundreds of hours if I let it.

games.title: Raft
games.review: A survival game that starts with nothing but a plank and a hook. Perfect co-op material.

games.title: Firewatch
games.review: A short, atmospheric walking sim by Campo Santo. The art direction alone is worth the price of admission.

games.title: Tomb Raider
games.review: The 2013 reboot that modernized Lara Croft. Heard the survival arc hits hard.

games.title: Ori and the Will of the Wisps
games.review: The sequel to Blind Forest, universally praised for tightening the combat. The art is simply stunning.

games.title: This War of Mine
games.review: A war game told from the perspective of civilians, not soldiers. Not looking forward to the emotional weight, but I need to play it.

games.title: Nine Sols
games.review: A Taopunk Metroidvania with a parry-heavy combat system that borrows from Sekiro. Everything about this is aimed squarely at my taste.

topPicks.blurb: The most rewarding game I have ever played. Every boss a mountain, every climb unforgettable.
The gold standard for open-world RPGs. Even the side quests outclass most games' main stories.
Night City feels alive. Phantom Liberty elevated the whole package into something I still think about.
Stunning art, brutal-but-fair combat, and lore deeper than most AAA worlds. The indie benchmark.
Redefining what an RPG can be. Every decision feels like it matters — and it actually does.

achievements.title: All Endings Unlocked
achievements.desc: Experienced every path Night City offers

achievements.title: Dream No More
achievements.desc: True ending — defeated the Nightmare King

achievements.title: Full Completion
achievements.desc: 100% of base game + both expansions cleared

achievements.title: Legend of Legends
achievements.desc: Achieved max connection rating in every region
